description: Closed-form golden for the refresh-path conformance fixture coupled_refresh_regrid.esm (CONFORMANCE_SPEC.md §5.10). The forcing is piecewise-constant across the cadence segments, so the IN-MODEL regridded forcing arrays and the integrated trajectory are exact. `native_fields` are the OFFLINE loader inputs on the coarse 6-cell native grid; `regridded_fields` are the analytic result of the in-model conservative 2:1 area-weighted coarsening onto the 3-cell sim grid (each target cell j = mean of native cells 2j-1, 2j, i.e. F_tgt[j] = sum_i W[i,j]*F_src[i] with W the const weight matrix in `regrid.weights`); `trajectory` is the segmented closed-form integral. All three simulation bindings reproduce regridded_fields (regrid band) and trajectory (trajectory band). The regrid is an ordinary in-model coupling contraction — there is NO regrid seam / RegridApplier (removed as obsolete in v0.8.0).

regrid.comment: The 2:1 area-weighted coarsening is expressed IN-MODEL as the const weight matrix W[i,j] (i=1..6 source cells, j=1..3 target cells): F_tgt[j] = sum_i W[i,j]*F_src[i]. Source cells are 6 unit intervals [k,k+1]x[0,1]; target (sim) cells are 3 width-2 intervals [2j-2,2j]x[0,1]. Equal overlap areas => W[i,j] = 0.5 for the two source cells i in target cell j, else 0 => target[j] = mean(source[2j-1], source[2j]). The distinct paired native values (e.g. 0 and 2 -> 1) make the averaging load-bearing: an identity pass-through would FAIL this golden. NOTE: no regrid seam — W is a const observed in the model and the contraction is the RHS itself.

trajectory.comment: Rate per segment r_k[j] = scale_tgt[j]*F_tgt_k[j]: scale_tgt=[1,2,3]; F_tgt_0=[1,1,1] -> r0=[1,2,3]; F_tgt_1=[2,2,2] -> r1=[2,4,6]; F_tgt_2=[3,3,3] -> r2=[3,6,9]. c(t)=sum_{k: anchor_k<t} r_k over the unit segments; d(t)=integral of c, where over unit segment [k,k+1] the integral is c(k)+r_k/2.
